{{Short description|Italian film festival in 2014}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox Film festival |name = 71st Venice International Film Festival |image = VeniceFilmFestival71poster.jpg |caption = Festival poster |opening = ''[[Birdman (film)|Birdman]]'' |closing = ''[[The Golden Era (film)|The Golden Era]]'' |location = [[Venice]], [[Italy]] |founded = 1932 |awards = [[Golden Lion]]:
''[[A Pigeon Sat on a Branch Reflecting on Existence]]'' |artistic_director= [[Alberto Barbera]] |host = [[Luisa Ranieri]] |number = |date = 27 August – 6 September 2014 |website = [http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/ Website] | chronology = Venice Film Festival | preceded_by = [[70th Venice International Film Festival|70th]] | followed_by = [[72nd Venice International Film Festival|72nd]] }} The '''71st annual [[Venice Film Festival|Venice International Film Festival]]''' , was held from 27 August to 6 September 2014, at [[Venice Lido]] in [[Italy]].{{cite web|url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/71st-festival/line-up/ |title=71st Venice International Film Festival Line-Up |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141007010534/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/71st-festival/line-up/ |archive-date=7 October 2014 }} French composer [[Alexandre Desplat]] was the jury president for the main competition.{{cite web |date=23 June 2014 |title=French Film Composer Alexandre Desplat To Head Venice Film Festival Jury |url=https://variety.com/2014/film/news/french-film-composer-alexandre-desplat-to-head-venice-film-festival-jury-1201241763/ |access-date=23 June 2014 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |archive-date=17 April 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180417105531/https://variety.com/2014/film/news/french-film-composer-alexandre-desplat-to-head-venice-film-festival-jury-1201241763/ |url-status=live }} Italian actress [[Luisa Ranieri]] hosted the opening and closing nights of the festival.{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/news/08-05.html?back=true |title=Luisa Ranieri to be the host of the 71st Venice Film Festival |access-date=28 August 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714133638/http://labiennale.org/en/cinema/news/08-05.html?back=true |archive-date=14 July 2014 |url-status=dead }} The [[Golden Lion]] was awarded to ''[[A Pigeon Sat on a Branch Reflecting on Existence]]'' by [[Roy Andersson]].{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-29098103 |title=Roy Andersson film scoops Venice Golden Lion award |access-date=2 May 2018 |work=BBC News |archive-date=7 September 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140907003505/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-29098103 |url-status=live }} American editor [[Thelma Schoonmaker]] and American filmmaker [[Frederick Wiseman]] were the recipients of the [[Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement]].{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/news/18-07.html |title=Thelma Schoonmaker and Frederick Wiseman Golden Lions for Lifetime Achievement |access-date=29 August 2014 |work=labiennale |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140903051855/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/news/18-07.html |archive-date=3 September 2014 |url-status=dead}} The festival poster paid tribute to [[François Truffaut]], as it featured the character of [[Antoine Doinel]] portrayed by [[Jean-Pierre Léaud]] from Truffaut's 1959 drama film ''[[The 400 Blows]]''.{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/venice-film-fest-unveils-poster-716764 |title=Venice Film Fest Unveils Poster for 71st Edition|access-date=28 August 2014 |work=The Hollywood Reporter}}{{cite web |url=http://www.swide.com/art-culture/latest-venice-film-festival-2014-news-from-inarritus-birdman-to-classics-lineup/2014/07/22 |title=Venice Film Festival 2014: What we know so far |access-date=28 August 2014 |work=Swide |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140810030855/http://www.swide.com/art-culture/latest-venice-film-festival-2014-news-from-inarritus-birdman-to-classics-lineup/2014/07/22 |archive-date=10 August 2014 |url-status=dead }} The festival opened with ''[[Birdman (film)|Birdman]]'' by [[Alejandro González Iñárritu|Alejandro G. Iñárritu]], and closed with ''[[The Golden Era (film)|The Golden Era]]'' by [[Ann Hui]].{{cite web |title=Birdman to hatch on opening night of Venice film festival |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2014/jul/10/birdman-venice-film-festival-opening-night |access-date=10 July 2014 |work=The Guardian |archive-date=11 July 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140711015042/http://www.theguardian.com/film/2014/jul/10/birdman-venice-film-festival-opening-night |url-status=live }}{{cite web |title=Venice Film Festival kicks off with Birdman |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-28963398 |access-date=28 August 2014 |work=BBC News |archive-date=28 August 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140828090614/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-28963398 |url-status=live }} ==Juries== === Main Competition (''Venezia 71'') === *[[Alexandre Desplat]], French composer - Jury President{{cite web|url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/71st-festival/juries/ |title=International Juries of the 71st Venice Film Festival |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141007005126/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/71st-festival/juries/ |archive-date=7 October 2014 }} *[[Joan Chen]], Chinese actress and director *[[Philip Gröning]], German director *[[Jessica Hausner]], Austrian filmmaker *[[Jhumpa Lahiri]], Indian novelist *[[Sandy Powell (costume designer)|Sandy Powell]], English costume designer *[[Tim Roth]], British actor *[[Elia Suleiman]], Palestinian filmmaker *[[Carlo Verdone]], Italian actor and director === Horizons (''Orizzonti'') === *[[Ann Hui]], Hong Kong director - Jury President{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2014/film/news/venice-film-festival-reveals-lineup-for-its-71st-edition-1201268122/|title=Venice Film Festival Reveals Lineup for Its 71st Edition |access-date=26 July 2014 |work=Variety}} *[[Moran Atias]], Israeli actress *[[Pernilla August]], Swedish actress and director *[[David Chase]], American writer and director *[[Mahamat-Saleh Haroun]], Chadian director * [[Roberto Minervini]], Italian director * Alin Taşçıyan, Turkish critic === Opera Prima (Venice Award for a Debut Film) === *[[Alice Rohrwacher]], Italian filmmaker -
